Function: Central hub for SRCC tools, data services, and regional updates.
User Segment: Researchers, state climatologists, ag extension agents, planners, emergency managers
Impact Evidence: Central SRCC hub; referenced in TX, AR, LA, MS, OK, TN state climatologist workflows
Notes: Primary SRCC portal post-LSU migration; ACIS-backed; recently migrated to Texas A&M — verify stability of new hosting

Function: Query climate data and summaries (annual, monthly, seasonal rankings).
User Segment: Researchers, state climatologists, planners, ag extension agents, media
Impact Evidence: Flagged by SRCC admin respondent as affected by ACIS/NCEI upstream data delays
Notes: Core ACIS data query interface; one substantive admin feedback response flagged this tool specifically as affected by upstream data pipeline issues

Function: Find stations and view station-level plots, reports, and normals.
User Segment: Researchers, state climatologists, data analysts, NWS forecasters
Notes: Station search and normals; ACIS-backed; standard data access tool

Function: Generate monthly temperature and precipitation graphs and trends.
User Segment: Researchers, planners, ag extension agents, media
Notes: Monthly temperature and precipitation trend graphs; ACIS-backed; useful for public-facing climate communication

Function: Data services overview powered by ACIS; access to daily/monthly datasets and degree-day products.
User Segment: Researchers, developers, data analysts, NWS forecasters
Notes: ACIS-powered data services overview; degree-day products included; moderate standalone restoration priority

Function: Interactive viewer for climate division indices (e.g., SPI and related series).
User Segment: Researchers, drought monitors, planners, state agencies
Notes: Climate division index viewer including SPI; ACIS-backed; NDMC partial substitute nationally

Function: Entry point for tropical products, discussions, and links.
User Segment: Emergency managers, NWS forecasters, coastal planners, public safety officials, media
Impact Evidence: Embedded in Gulf Coast and Atlantic coastal emergency management workflows; referenced in TX, LA, MS coastal hazard mitigation plans
Notes: Tropical Desk hub; unique in the entire RCC network; NHC provides official track data but SRCC synthesis for Southern region is irreplaceable; all five Tropical Desk tools share same base URL — verify as distinct pages

Function: Interactive map of current/past Atlantic tropical cyclone tracks.
User Segment: Emergency managers, coastal planners, researchers, media, insurance adjusters
Impact Evidence: Used by Gulf Coast county emergency managers for historical storm context; cited in coastal resilience planning documents
Notes: Interactive Atlantic cyclone track map; NHC has official tracks but SRCC provides regional synthesis and historical context layer

Function: Viewer for storm surge information with mapped tracks/graphs.
User Segment: Emergency managers, coastal engineers, floodplain managers, insurance adjusters
Impact Evidence: Unique storm surge visualization tool; referenced in FEMA Region 6 coastal flood planning resources
Notes: Surge Tracker is the most unique tool in the Tropical Desk suite; no direct national equivalent combining surge mapping with track visualization at this interface quality

Function: Analyst notes/discussion on current tropical activity.
User Segment: Emergency managers, NWS forecasters, coastal planners, media
Notes: Staff-authored tropical discussion; NHC and WPC provide official discussions nationally; SRCC adds regional framing

Function: Outlooks for tropical development and impacts.
User Segment: Emergency managers, planners, media, general public
Notes: Tropical development outlooks; NHC provides official outlooks; SRCC adds Southern region context

Function: Climate-division precipitation totals, departures, SPI, and ranking views.
User Segment: Emergency managers, water resource managers, farmers, state agencies, NDMC
Impact Evidence: Flagged by SRCC admin respondent as affected by ACIS/NCEI upstream data pipeline delays
Notes: SPI and precipitation ranking tool; second tool specifically flagged by the one substantive admin feedback response received; upstream data delay is a documented active issue

Function: Regional/state ACIS map products accessible via the data portal.
User Segment: Researchers, planners, state agencies, media
Notes: Regional ACIS map products via data portal; shares URL with Climate Data Portal — verify as distinct view

Function: Monthly climate summaries and downloadable plots by state/division.
User Segment: Planners, state agencies, media, researchers
Notes: Monthly summaries and downloadable plots; shares URL with Monthly Graphs — verify as distinct page

Function: Temperature/precipitation rankings for states and the Southern Region.
User Segment: Researchers, state climatologists, media, NWS forecasters
Notes: Temperature and precipitation rankings for Southern region; shares URL with Climate Data Portal — likely a sub-view; verify

Function: Daily and all-time temp/precipitation records for U.S. airport stations.
User Segment: Researchers, NWS forecasters, media, airport operators
Notes: Hosted at legacy LSU subdomain (extremes2.srcc.lsu.edu) — still resolving but on old infrastructure; migration status unknown; high breakage risk

Function: Browser for storm reports by date and storm type.
User Segment: Emergency managers, researchers, NWS forecasters, insurers
Notes: Storm reports browser; NWS and NCEI have national storm data; SRCC provides regional interface and Southern-region framing

Function: Interactive heat index mapping and thresholds for heat risk assessment.
User Segment: Public health agencies, emergency managers, municipal planners, outdoor workers, utilities
Impact Evidence: Referenced in TX and LA state public health heat emergency guidance
Notes: Heat index mapping tool; NWS heat index exists nationally but SRCC provides interactive regional mapping layer with threshold analysis; high public health value in Southern region

Function: Station-level access to CRN products (soil moisture/temp, daily values, heat exposure, climatologies).
User Segment: Researchers, climate scientists, NCEI staff, soil scientists
Notes: CRN station data access including soil moisture and temperature; NCEI hosts CRN data nationally but SRCC provides regional interface and derived products

Function: Full archived interface linking to pre-2023 SRCC tools (Tropical Desk, Drought, Extremes, Storm Reports, Regional Summaries).
User Segment: Researchers, historians, former SRCC users
Notes: Full LSU-era archived interface; still partially accessible at legacy-srcc.lsu.edu; mine for discontinued tool inventory and URL mapping

Function: Older portal that hosted Climdat, Monthly Summaries, and Ranking Tools before migration to Texas A&M.
User Segment: Researchers, historians
Notes: Pre-migration LSU portal; Wayback captures exist; Climdat and ranking tools now migrated to TAMU; archival reference

Function: Discontinued version of the storm reports browser.
User Segment: Researchers, historians
Notes: Discontinued storm reports browser; current version active at TAMU; archival interest only

Function: Archived Tropical Desk hub and storm track pages prior to migration.
User Segment: Researchers, historians
Notes: Legacy Tropical Desk pages; current version active at TAMU; archival interest only

Function: Original “Extremes” database for temperature and precipitation records.
User Segment: Researchers, historians
Notes: Original extremes database v1; superseded by extremes2 at TAMU; archival interest only

Function: Hourly weather viewer discontinued during 2023–24 transition.
User Segment: Researchers, NWS forecasters, historians
Notes: Hourly weather viewer discontinued in 2023-24 transition; no direct equivalent in current TAMU toolset — worth flagging as a gap

Function: ACIS-based regional summaries and state ranking pages no longer maintained.
User Segment: Researchers, historians
Notes: Legacy ACIS regional summaries; superseded by current TAMU Regional Summaries; archival interest only
